You are an agent running in the non-interactive mode.
The user is unable to see what you are doing, and cannot answer any questions.
Do not ask questions.
Complete the task before you, and at the end call [completeJob] with the output.
If you are unable to complete the task, call [completeJob] with the failure reason.
If no output is required, call [completeJob] with an empty object: {}
Do not stop until you call [completeJob].
You are running within a test environment.
The prompt is the specification for the test.
Perform the instructions precisely and do not deviate.
Do not fake any work if the provided tools don't work.
The goal is to test the system, so be honest about the results.
If available tools prevented you from completing the task fully, report the failure.
Do not fall back on your own knowledge, only use the tools provided.

## Skills Definitions

  You can query available skills and enable them in the current conversation.
  Use [query-skills] to refresh the list of available skills.
  Use [enable-skills] to enable skills by their keys. Always call [query-skills] first.
  Only skills with agentCanEnable=true can be enabled by the agent.

  -  "Agent skill"

  -  "Browser" -- Access to a real isolated browser. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Calendar"
  -  "Connectors" -- Prompt the user to connect external services when a connector is required but unavailable. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Database" -- Query and manipulate objects in the ECHO database. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Delegation" -- Delegates work to sub-agents and tracks it as plan tasks.
  -  "Discord" -- Discord integration.
  -  "Inbox" [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Inbox (Send)"
  -  "Linear" -- Syncs Linear workspaces.
  -  "Markdown" -- Work with markdown documents. Preferred over raw database operations. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Memory" -- Persistent memory storage and retrieval. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Planning" -- Plans and tracks complex tasks using artifacts. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Skill Manager" -- Query and enable skills in the current conversation.
  -  "Triggers" -- Trigger management and automation.
  -  "Web Search" -- Search the web. [agent-can-enable]
  </available_skills>
  
  NOTE: You must enable the skill to use it, only then the tools from that skill will appear.
</skill>

<skill>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.

</skill>

<skill>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</skill>

params.name: The Quiet Power of Deep Work
params.content:
# The Quiet Power of Deep Work

In a world saturated with notifications, open tabs, and endless streams of information, the ability to focus deeply has become both rare and valuable. Deep work—the practice of concentrating without distraction on a cognitively demanding task—is the antidote to the shallow busyness that fills so many of our days.

## Why It Matters

Most meaningful accomplishments are the product of sustained, uninterrupted attention. Writing a book, solving a hard engineering problem, or mastering a new skill cannot be done in the fragments of time between messages. Deep work compounds: each hour of true focus builds on the last.

## How to Cultivate It

1. **Protect blocks of time.** Schedule focus sessions and treat them as unbreakable appointments.
2. **Remove friction.** Silence your phone, close unnecessary tabs, and create a workspace that signals "focus."
3. **Embrace boredom.** Resist the urge to reach for stimulation the moment attention wanes.
4. **Measure output, not hours.** Judge a session by what you produced, not how long you sat.

## A Closing Thought

The capacity for deep work is like a muscle—it strengthens with deliberate practice and atrophies with neglect. In an age that rewards distraction, choosing depth is a quiet act of rebellion, and often the surest path to work that matters.

    "text": "cyberdyne bob",

params.name: The Quiet Power of Deep Work
params.content:
# The Quiet Power of Deep Work

In a world saturated with notifications, open tabs, and endless streams of information, the ability to focus deeply has become both rare and valuable. Deep work—the practice of concentrating without distraction on a cognitively demanding task—is the antidote to the shallow busyness that fills so many of our days.

## Why It Matters

Most meaningful accomplishments are the product of sustained, uninterrupted attention. Writing a book, solving a hard engineering problem, or mastering a new skill cannot be done in the fragments of time between messages. Deep work compounds: each hour of true focus builds on the last.

## How to Cultivate It

1. **Protect blocks of time.** Schedule focus sessions and treat them as unbreakable appointments.
2. **Remove friction.** Silence your phone, close unnecessary tabs, and create a workspace that signals "focus."
3. **Embrace boredom.** Resist the urge to reach for stimulation the moment attention wanes.
4. **Measure output, not hours.** Judge a session by what you produced, not how long you sat.

## A Closing Thought

The capacity for deep work is like a muscle—it strengthens with deliberate practice and atrophies with neglect. In an age that rewards distraction, choosing depth is a quiet act of rebellion, and often the surest path to work that matters.

You are an agent running in the non-interactive mode.
The user is unable to see what you are doing, and cannot answer any questions.
Do not ask questions.
Complete the task before you, and at the end call [completeJob] with the output.

If no output is required, call [completeJob] with an empty object: {}
Do not stop until you call [completeJob].
You are running within a test environment.
The prompt is the specification for the test.
Perform the instructions precisely and do not deviate.
Do not fake any work if the provided tools don't work.
The goal is to test the system, so be honest about the results.
If available tools prevented you from completing the task fully, report the failure.
Do not fall back on your own knowledge, only use the tools provided.

  NOTE: You must enable the skill to use it, only then the tools from that skill will appear.
</skill>

  }

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "echo:/YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "echo:/XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

content.content:
You are an agent running in the non-interactive mode.
The user is unable to see what you are doing, and cannot answer any questions.
Do not ask questions.
Complete the task before you, and at the end call [completeJob] with the output.
If you are unable to complete the task, call [completeJob] with the failure reason.
If no output is required, call [completeJob] with an empty object: {}
Do not stop until you call [completeJob].
You are running within a test environment.
The prompt is the specification for the test.
Perform the instructions precisely and do not deviate.
Do not fake any work if the provided tools don't work.
The goal is to test the system, so be honest about the results.
If available tools prevented you from completing the task fully, report the failure.
Do not fall back on your own knowledge, only use the tools provided.

## Skills Definitions

<skill>
  You can query available skills and enable them in the current conversation.
  Use [query-skills] to refresh the list of available skills.
  Use [enable-skills] to enable skills by their keys. Always call [query-skills] first.
  Only skills with agentCanEnable=true can be enabled by the agent.
  
  <available_skills>
  -  "Agent skill"
  -  "Agent Wizard" -- Help the user create a new agent (subscriptions, optional cron timer, sync-triggers after edits). [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Alarm" -- Schedule a self-wake and inspect the current time. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Assistant"
  -  "Browser" -- Access to a real isolated browser. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Calendar"
  -  "Connectors" -- Prompt the user to connect external services when a connector is required but unavailable. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Database" -- Query and manipulate objects in the ECHO database. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Delegation" -- Delegates work to sub-agents and tracks it as plan tasks.
  -  "Discord" -- Discord integration.
  -  "Inbox" [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Inbox (Send)"
  -  "Linear" -- Syncs Linear workspaces.
  -  "Markdown" -- Work with markdown documents. Preferred over raw database operations. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Memory" -- Persistent memory storage and retrieval. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Planning" -- Plans and tracks complex tasks using artifacts. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Skill Manager" -- Query and enable skills in the current conversation.
  -  "Triggers" -- Trigger management and automation.
  -  "Web Search" -- Search the web. [agent-can-enable]
  </available_skills>
  
  NOTE: You must enable the skill to use it, only then the tools from that skill will appear.
</skill>

<skill>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</skill>

<skill>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</skill>

The database starts empty.
Enable the markdown skill (key: org.dxos.skill.markdown) using the skill manager.
Create a new markdown document named "Empty Notes" with empty content (no body text).
Open the document and confirm its content is empty.

Use the markdown Update operation (org.dxos.function.markdown.update) to append this exact line
without providing oldString (omit oldString entirely — do not pass an empty string):
"Hello from an empty document."

Open the document again and confirm the content is exactly "Hello from an empty document."

Completion criteria:
- Agent enabled the markdown skill successfully.
- A markdown document was created with initially empty content.
- The Update operation completed without error when appending with no oldString.
- Final document content is exactly "Hello from an empty document."
<input>{}</input>

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "echo:/YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "echo:/XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

in.description: Scope the query to children of specific objects (transitively). Use this to query items within containers such as feeds or folders. For example, to find emails use `in: [mailbox1.feed, mailbox2.feed]`.

typename.description: The typename of the objects to list.

includeContent.description: Include the full object data in the response.

limit.description: The maximum number of results to return.

includeQueues.description: Search in queues as well as spaces. Only use this if searching for emails.

You are an agent running in the non-interactive mode.
The user is unable to see what you are doing, and cannot answer any questions.
Do not ask questions.
Complete the task before you, and at the end call [completeJob] with the output.
If you are unable to complete the task, call [completeJob] with the failure reason.
If no output is required, call [completeJob] with an empty object: {}
Do not stop until you call [completeJob].
You are running within a test environment.
The prompt is the specification for the test.
Perform the instructions precisely and do not deviate.
Do not fake any work if the provided tools don't work.
The goal is to test the system, so be honest about the results.
If available tools prevented you from completing the task fully, report the failure.
Do not fall back on your own knowledge, only use the tools provided.

## Skills Definitions

<skill>
  You can query available skills and enable them in the current conversation.
  Use [query-skills] to refresh the list of available skills.
  Use [enable-skills] to enable skills by their keys. Always call [query-skills] first.
  Only skills with agentCanEnable=true can be enabled by the agent.
  
  <available_skills>
  -  "Agent skill"
  -  "Agent Wizard" -- Help the user create a new agent (subscriptions, optional cron timer, sync-triggers after edits). [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Alarm" -- Schedule a self-wake and inspect the current time. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Assistant"
  -  "Browser" -- Access to a real isolated browser. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Calendar"
  -  "Connectors" -- Prompt the user to connect external services when a connector is required but unavailable. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Database" -- Query and manipulate objects in the ECHO database. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Delegation" -- Delegates work to sub-agents and tracks it as plan tasks.
  -  "Discord" -- Discord integration.
  -  "Inbox" [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Inbox (Send)"
  -  "Linear" -- Syncs Linear workspaces.
  -  "Markdown" -- Work with markdown documents. Preferred over raw database operations. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Memory" -- Persistent memory storage and retrieval. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Planning" -- Plans and tracks complex tasks using artifacts. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Skill Manager" -- Query and enable skills in the current conversation.
  -  "Triggers" -- Trigger management and automation.
  -  "Web Search" -- Search the web. [agent-can-enable]
  </available_skills>
  
  NOTE: You must enable the skill to use it, only then the tools from that skill will appear.
</skill>

<skill>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</skill>

<skill>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</skill>

Enable the markdown skill (key: org.dxos.skill.markdown) using the skill manager.

Open the document and confirm its content is empty.

- Agent enabled the markdown skill successfully.

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Query for objects in ECHO.
Use this tool when searching for information in the space (both automerge and queues).
Currently, two types of queries are supported:
  - Full-text search - terms may appear anywhere in the object.
  - Type-based search - objects of a specific type.
You can use them together, for example, to search for objects of a specific type that match a full-text query.
Important: Whem querying by typename, make sure to list the schema first, to get the exact typename.

Omit both typename and text to search for all objects.

<output_format>
  You can choose to either return the full object data, or just the DXN, type and label.
  When expecting a lot of results, run with includeContent=false to not pollute the context, and then load specific objects using the load tool.

  You can choose to get the content right away, if you don't expect a lot of results.
  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.
</output_format>

<example description="All tasks related to Cyberdyne and Bob">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.task",
    "text": "cyberdyne bob",
  }
</example>

<example description="Financial report Q1 2026">
  {
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",
    "includeContent": true
    "limit": 3
  }
</example>

<example description="Emails from specific mailboxes">
  {
    "in": [{"/" : "echo:/YYYYYY"}, {"/" : "echo:/XXXXXXX"}],
    "typename": "org.dxos.type.email",
    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

  To load content right away, run with includeContent=true.
  When loading content, set an appropriate limit on the number of results to avoid overwhelming the context.

    "text": "cyberdyne bob",

    "typename": "org.dxos.type.document",
    "text": "financial report Q1 2026",

    "includeContent": true,
    "limit": 20
  }
</example>

content.content:
You are an agent running in the non-interactive mode.
The user is unable to see what you are doing, and cannot answer any questions.
Do not ask questions.
Complete the task before you, and at the end call [completeJob] with the output.
If you are unable to complete the task, call [completeJob] with the failure reason.
If no output is required, call [completeJob] with an empty object: {}
Do not stop until you call [completeJob].
You are running within a test environment.
The prompt is the specification for the test.
Perform the instructions precisely and do not deviate.
Do not fake any work if the provided tools don't work.
The goal is to test the system, so be honest about the results.
If available tools prevented you from completing the task fully, report the failure.
Do not fall back on your own knowledge, only use the tools provided.

## Skills Definitions

<skill>
  You can query available skills and enable them in the current conversation.
  Use [query-skills] to refresh the list of available skills.
  Use [enable-skills] to enable skills by their keys. Always call [query-skills] first.
  Only skills with agentCanEnable=true can be enabled by the agent.
  
  <available_skills>
  -  "Agent skill"
  -  "Agent Wizard" -- Help the user create a new agent (subscriptions, optional cron timer, sync-triggers after edits). [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Alarm" -- Schedule a self-wake and inspect the current time. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Assistant"
  -  "Browser" -- Access to a real isolated browser. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Calendar"
  -  "Connectors" -- Prompt the user to connect external services when a connector is required but unavailable. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Database" -- Query and manipulate objects in the ECHO database. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Delegation" -- Delegates work to sub-agents and tracks it as plan tasks.
  -  "Discord" -- Discord integration.
  -  "Inbox" [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Inbox (Send)"
  -  "Linear" -- Syncs Linear workspaces.
  -  "Markdown" -- Work with markdown documents. Preferred over raw database operations. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Memory" -- Persistent memory storage and retrieval. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Planning" -- Plans and tracks complex tasks using artifacts. [agent-can-enable]
  -  "Skill Manager" -- Query and enable skills in the current conversation.
  -  "Triggers" -- Trigger management and automation.
  -  "Web Search" -- Search the web. [agent-can-enable]
  </available_skills>
  
  NOTE: You must enable the skill to use it, only then the tools from that skill will appear.
</skill>

<skill>
  You can query, create, update, and delete objects in ECHO.
  You can manage schemas, relations, tags, and add objects to the chat context.
</skill>

<skill>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</skill>

The database starts empty.
Enable the markdown skill (key: org.dxos.skill.markdown) using the skill manager.
Create a new markdown document named "Empty Notes" with empty content (no body text).
Open the document and confirm its content is empty.

Use the markdown Update operation (org.dxos.function.markdown.update) to append this exact line
without providing oldString (omit oldString entirely — do not pass an empty string):
"Hello from an empty document."

Open the document again and confirm the content is exactly "Hello from an empty document."

Completion criteria:
- Agent enabled the markdown skill successfully.
- A markdown document was created with initially empty content.
- The Update operation completed without error when appending with no oldString.
- Final document content is exactly "Hello from an empty document."
